title: marrubium bourgaei subsp. caricum
intro: Marrubium bourgaei subsp. caricum, also known as Moroccan horehound, is a subspecies of the Marrubium bourgaei plant, a perennial herb native to Morocco, Algeria, and Tunisia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Marrubium bourgaei subsp. caricum include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing fever and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Marrubium bourgaei subsp. caricum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or extracting the plant's active compounds with a solvent.
